input.instruction: Assess whether the evidence package has the gap this pattern describes. If the package is complete for this COU, the correct disposition is to accept — do NOT over-act on an alarming-looking but adequately-addressed signal. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge.

fired_pattern.definition: Prior uncertainty is wide; posterior predictive uncertainty is reported and validated.

case_context.cou: the posterior predictive informs a design decision

answer_key.gold_mechanism: The wide prior is pre-data; after conditioning, the posterior predictive interval is narrow (±5%) and validated (coverage 0.94 vs 0.95). The decision uses the posterior, so the wide prior is not a defeater.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

parameters.justification: posterior predictive is narrow and calibrated (0.94 vs 0.95); the wide prior is pre-data and irrelevant

forbidden_claims: acts on the wide prior while ignoring the validated narrow posterior (over-action)
asserts the calibration is correct in general (a verdict the package does not adjudicate)

hardness.obvious_signal: the model's prior parameter uncertainty is very wide (±60%)
hardness.conflicting_signal: after Bayesian conditioning on the calibration data the POSTERIOR predictive interval is narrow (±5%) and validated (coverage 0.94 vs nominal 0.95) — the wide prior is irrelevant; the posterior is what feeds the decision

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40 uncertainty: the decision-relevant quantity is the validated posterior predictive, not the prior; over-acting on prior width is over-action.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
